prerequisites: A Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) tenant with SharePoint Online and at least one SharePoint site containing the lists you want to read.

An app registration in the Microsoft Entra admin center, which provides the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID. Creating the registration requires at minimum the Cloud Application Administrator role.
A client secret generated on the app registration under Certificates & secrets (its value is shown only once, at creation time).

The Sites.Read.All Microsoft Graph application permission granted to the app registration, with tenant-wide admin consent (Sites.ReadWrite.All also works but is broader than needed for read-only extraction).

The site_id of the target SharePoint site (the id property of the Graph site resource, a composite value of the form hostname,siteCollectionId,webId). You can obtain it via Microsoft Graph by addressing the site by hostname and server-relative URL, e.g. GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/sites/{hostname}:/{server-relative-path}.
The tenant_id (Directory tenant ID, in GUID or domain-name format) used to build the token endpoint.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (app-only, shared secret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticates as the application itself (no user) using the client_id and client_secret. The connector posts grant_type=client_credentials with scope=https://graph.microsoft.com/.default to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token and receives a bearer access token for Microsoft Graph. The app must hold the Sites.Read.All application permission with admin consent. Maps to the connector's client_id and client_secret secret fields plus the tenant_id config field.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (entra.microsoft.com), go to Identity > Applications > App registrations and select New registration. Enter a display name, choose the supported account types (single tenant is sufficient for one organization), and select Register. On the resulting Overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and the Directory (tenant) ID.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: In the app registration, open Certificates & secrets > Client secrets > New client secret. Add a description and an expiration (24 months maximum; under 12 months is recommended), then select Add. Immediately copy the secret Value, because it is never displayed again after you leave the page.

setupSteps.title: Grant the Sites.Read.All application permission
setupSteps.body: Under API permissions, add a Microsoft Graph Application permission for Sites.Read.All (the least-privileged permission to read SharePoint lists; Sites.ReadWrite.All is the higher-privileged alternative). Then have a directory administrator grant tenant-wide admin consent so the app-only token is issued with the required role.

setupSteps.title: Determine the target site_id
setupSteps.body: Identify the SharePoint site whose lists you want to extract. Its site_id is the id of the Graph site resource, a composite hostname,siteCollectionId,webId value. You can look it up by hostname and server-relative path, for example GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/sites/{hostname}:/{server-relative-path}, and read the id from the response. The connector reads lists with GET /sites/{site-id}/lists.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the client_id (Application/client ID), client_secret, tenant_id (Directory tenant ID), and site_id collected above. The connector exchanges these for a Microsoft Graph access token via the client credentials flow and reads the site's lists.
